sn47.41:1.1: At Sāvatthī. "Bhikkhus, dwell with your minds well established in the four establishments of mindfulness. Do not let the Deathless be lost on you.[n.179] Mā vo amataṁ panassa. Spk offers no help, but I take panassa to be an aorist of panassati. Woodward has apparently understood it as pan’ assa and translates, "But let not that be to you the Deathless" (KS 5:161). But pana here would be syntactically out of place.
sn47.41:1.2: "In what four? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu dwells contemplating the body in the body … feelings in feelings … mind in mind … phenomena in phenomena, ardent, clearly comprehending, mindful, having removed covetousness and displeasure in regard to the world. Dwell, bhikkhus, with your minds well established in these four establishments of mindfulness. Do not let the Deathless be lost on you."
